platforms.name: Steam

config_files.notes: GPU and display settings. Overwritten whenever the in-game Video settings menu is closed. Edit only while CS2 is fully closed.

Console variable file. Executed automatically on game start if +exec autoexec is present in launch options, or if autoexec.cfg exists in the cfg directory on modern CS2 builds. Create this file if it does not exist.

platforms.launch_options_path: Steam Library > Right-click CS2 > Properties > Launch Options

keys.notes: Exclusive fullscreen gives the lowest input latency via hardware flip presentation. Borderless adds ~1 frame of latency through Desktop Window Manager composition. Strongly recommended to use exclusive fullscreen for competitive play.

keys.effect: CPU simulation quality (ragdolls, debris, particle density): 0=low, 1=medium, 2=high.
keys.notes: Level 2 provides no meaningful visual benefit in competitive play and adds CPU overhead during intense rounds. Not recommended above 1.
Competitive players universally disable MSAA for maximum FPS. Even 2x MSAA has a measurable frametime cost in large-scale server games.

console_vars.notes: Setting 10 above monitor refresh rate (e.g., 251 for 240 Hz) ensures the pipeline stays full without uncapped thermal load. Do not use 0 — this causes GPU overheat during low-complexity map areas such as spawn zones.

console_vars.effect: FPS overlay: 0=off, 1=FPS only, 4=FPS + frame time graph.
Forces all map assets to preload before game start: 0=off, 1=on.
console_vars.notes: Eliminates mid-match texture/audio pop-in at the cost of slower map load times. Highly recommended on systems with 16 GB+ RAM. May cause RAM pressure on 8 GB systems on complex maps.
Multi-threaded mode (2) improves CPU utilisation on quad-core and higher CPUs. Auto (-1) typically selects 2 on modern hardware. Explicit 2 ensures it is not overridden by GPU driver defaults.

console_vars.effect: Maximum instanced draw calls per frame. Lower values reduce CPU overhead at the cost of detail density.
console_vars.notes: Default is 512. Reducing to 128 on CPU-limited systems can recover 5-15% CPU frame time in high-player-count servers.

launch_options.effect: Disables joystick/gamepad input subsystem. Reduces startup time and memory footprint on non-controller setups.

launch_options.notes: Not always required on modern CS2 builds — the game auto-executes autoexec.cfg if present. Including it explicitly is harmless and ensures the file always runs.

launch_options.effect: Sets process priority to High in Windows Task Manager.
launch_options.notes: Can cause audio dropouts and system instability by starving background Windows processes. The benefit is marginal even on systems with many background apps. Not recommended.

engine_overrides.behaviour: Fully rewritten by the in-game Video settings menu on every close. Manual edits will be lost if the user opens and saves any video setting.
engine_overrides.action: Warn the user to edit cs2_video.txt only while CS2 is fully closed. For truly persistent display preferences, instruct the user to set them in-game first, then make additional tweaks to the file.

engine_overrides.behaviour: VAC does not scan config files or autoexec.cfg. All keys and console variables in this profile are VAC-safe. No modifications to game binary files are recommended.
engine_overrides.action: Safe to apply all recommended settings. Do not recommend cheat-adjacent cvars such as sv_cheats-gated commands — these are server-controlled and cannot be applied by clients.

notes: CS2 replaced CS:GO in September 2023. All CSGO-era config documentation referencing the Windows registry or CSGO-specific paths is obsolete.
The in-game Boost Player Contrast setting (Settings > Video > Advanced) is equivalent to the old cl_contrast console variable. It is not accessible via config file — set in-game.

NVIDIA Reflex is available in CS2's Advanced Video settings. It significantly reduces input latency (measured in mouse-to-pixel latency). Enabled + Boost is recommended on all NVIDIA GPUs; has no effect on AMD/Intel.
AMD Anti-Lag+ is available for AMD RX 7000 series and newer through the AMD Software Adrenalin Edition overlay.
